text.title: go-concurrency Core Rules
text.summary: Nine testable rules covering channel ownership, explicit exit, fan-out, sizing, locking, and CI invariants.

rule.statement: The goroutine that writes to a channel is the one that closes it. No exceptions.
rule.rationale: Closing from a non-writer goroutine causes panic when the writer is mid-send. Channel ownership rule eliminates that class of bug.

rule.statement: Every goroutine must have an explicit exit condition: ctx.Done(), channel close, or WaitGroup.Done path. Never write bare `go func()` without a shutdown mechanism.
rule.rationale: Bare goroutines leak; goleak catches them, but the rule prevents the bug at write time.

rule.statement: Use `errgroup.WithContext` over hand-rolled WaitGroup + errCh for fan-out — it cancels siblings on first error automatically.
rule.rationale: Hand-rolled error fan-out routinely forgets to cancel siblings; errgroup encodes the right pattern.

rule.statement: Bound concurrency with a buffered semaphore sized to downstream capacity (DB pool size, API rate limit), not to runtime.NumCPU().
rule.rationale: NumCPU is unrelated to downstream throughput; sizing by CPUs overshoots DB pools and rate limits, causing timeouts.

rule.statement: Use sync.RWMutex for read-heavy caches. Use sync.Map only when keys are write-once and reads dominate overwhelmingly; otherwise RWMutex + map is more predictable.
rule.rationale: sync.Map is optimized for narrow workloads; RWMutex is simpler, well-understood, and benches well for typical caches.

rule.statement: Always call wg.Add(1) before launching the goroutine, never inside it.
rule.rationale: wg.Add inside the body races with wg.Wait — a wait may return before Add is called.

rule.statement: In select statements, always include a `<-ctx.Done()` case to ensure graceful cancellation. A select without ctx.Done can block indefinitely even when the parent context is cancelled.
rule.rationale: Without ctx.Done branch, cancellation is silent. Adding it costs one case and prevents shutdown hangs.

rule.statement: All tests for concurrency-heavy packages must pass `go test -race -count=10 ./...` before merge. Never skip the race detector.
rule.rationale: Race detector is non-deterministic; repeated runs surface races that a single run misses.

rule.statement: For packages with concurrency primitives, add a TestMain that calls `goleak.VerifyTestMain(m)`. This detects goroutine leaks at the end of every test run.
rule.rationale: Forgotten goroutines accumulate; goleak prevents leaks from shipping.

rule.statement: All goroutines have explicit exit, channels owned by their writers, errgroup used for fan-out, CI runs -race -count>=10, goleak verifies TestMain.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion target for the decision tree's positive branch.

rule.statement: No goroutines or channels are introduced — the methodology does not apply.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion target for the decision tree's negative branch.
